slides.title: Thinking, Feelings, and Imagination
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 9: Thinking, Feelings, and Imagination. Welcome to the last unit of Year 3! This unit is all about the world inside your head — your thinking, your feelings, and your imagination. You will learn words for ideas and emotions, like idea, sense, reason, sadness, and experience. You will learn how to imagine new things, how to make a choice, and how to suggest ideas kindly to your friends. You will read about children in a coastal East African town who fill a special Box of Ideas with their dreams and hopes. By the end, you will be able to talk about what you think and feel, and use your imagination to write your own ideas down. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use abstract nouns like idea, reason, experience, and sadness to talk about thoughts and feelings
Say what you imagine and describe pictures in your mind
Give a reason for something using because
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use abstract nouns like idea, reason, experience, and sadness to talk about thoughts and feelings. you will also say what you imagine and describe pictures in your mind. you will also give a reason for something using because. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: idea, imagine, reason, purpose, choice, sense. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: The Box of Ideas at School
Feelings Are Not Bad or Good
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes The Box of Ideas at School and Feelings Are Not Bad or Good. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Abstract Nouns
bullets: An abstract noun is a word for something you cannot see, touch, or hold — like a feeling, a quality, or an idea. Words like idea, experience, sadness, and...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Abstract Nouns. An abstract noun is a word for something you cannot see, touch, or hold — like a feeling, a quality, or an idea. Words like idea, experience, sadness, and kindness are abstract nouns. They let you talk about what is inside your mind and heart. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1 — My Idea for the Box
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Share an Idea. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
